title: Battle of Mizra
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Battle |prev=*[[Sith incursion at King's Galquek]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[Sith incursion at Corphelion]] *[[Battle of Gap Nine|Sith incursion at Gap Nine]] |conc= |next= |image= |name=Battle of Mizra |conflict=[[New Sith Wars]] |date=[[1466 BBY]] |place= |result=[[New Sith]] victory |side1=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side2=New Sith |side3= |side4= |commanders1=A [[Unidentified Jedi coordinator|Jedi coordinator]]†[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*At least half a million Republic [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] *At least hundreds of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] |forces2=*An [[Army/Legends|army]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' **"Scores" of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] **"Scores" of [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]] ***[[Hssiss (speeder)|''Hssiss'']] ***[[Ng'ok (speeder)|''Ng'ok'']] ***[[Sleeth (speeder)|''Sleeth'']] **A [[Unidentified Sith sniper|Sith sniper]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*More than half a million Republic troops *The Jedi coordinator *Hundreds of Jedi captured |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|The Battle of Mizra (1466 B.B.Y.) saw the victory of a massive Sith army over the Jedi. It was immortalized by the poet Felloux, who described scores of Sith speeders bearing scores of Sith Lords ''[…]'' The Sith Lords' drawn lightsabers illuminated the ash-blackened sky, and their whining repulsorlift chariots drowned out the screams of the dying.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|}} The '''Battle of Mizra''' was an engagement of the [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]]-long [[New Sith Wars]] that took place in [[1466 BBY]]. It pitted a force of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], accompanied by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and led by a [[Unidentified Jedi coordinator|Jedi coordinator]], against a massive [[Army/Legends|army]] of the [[New Sith]] that featured many [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] riding [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]]. At one point during a Jedi retreat, a [[Unidentified Sith sniper|Sith sniper]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Jedi coordinator—who had been using the [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|Force ability]] to improve the combat performance of the Republic force—causing a rout that saw the deaths of more than half a million Republic troops and capture of hundreds of Jedi. The defeat at the Battle of Mizra served as a turning point in the New Sith Wars, resulting in significant loss of Republic [[Politics/Legends|political]] control over the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and a rise in power for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. [[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] after the battle, it was mentioned in documents such as the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'', the Sith text ''[[The Rule of Two]]'', and a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|chronicle]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. The battle was also immortalized in a description by the [[Poet/Legends|poet]] [[Felloux]]. ==Prelude== The [[New Sith Wars]] were a conflict waged from [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[New Sith]], a group of members of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tradition of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]]. Initially set back by numerous instances of Sith [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]], by [[1500 BBY]] the Republic had managed to draft sufficiently many [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] to counter Sith offensives at the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] celestial bodies [[Battle of Gap Nine|Gap Nine]] and [[Sith incursion at King's Galquek|King's Galquek]] as well as the astronomical object [[Sith incursion at Corphelion|Corphelion]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], however, the Sith allied with members of the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] and [[Hiitian/Legends|Hiitian]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. ==The battle== {{Quote|At the Battle of Mizra, the death of the Jedi coordinator to a Sith sniper turned the Jedi retreat into a full-scale slaughter.|Jedi Master Sabla-Mandibu, ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''|}} [[1466 BBY]] saw the occurrence of the Battle of Mizra, which shared its name with the [[Mizra system/Legends|Mizra system]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. A force of at least half a million Republic troops, accompanied by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]—members of the [[Jedi Order]], which was the ideological anti-thesis of the Sith—and led by a [[Unidentified Jedi coordinator|Jedi coordinator]] faced a massive Sith [[Army/Legends|army]] at a location within an [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The latter force included numerous [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] riding [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]]—such as [[Hssiss (speeder)|''Hssiss'']], [[Ng'ok (speeder)|''Ng'ok'']], and [[Sleeth (speeder)|''Sleeth'']]—each of which was named after a [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] creature whose nature corresponded to the rider's character. In the battle, in which the Jedi coordinator was employing the [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|Force ability]] to boost the combat performance of the Republic soldiers and hinder that of the Sith, the latter faction nevertheless emerged victorious. At one point, the Jedi mounted a retreat, at which point a [[Unidentified Sith sniper|Sith sniper]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Jedi coordinator. As a result, the retreat turned into a slaughter, with more than half a million Republic troops perishing in the battle and hundreds of Jedi becoming captured and subsequently converting to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. ==Aftermath== ===Remembrances grave and flippant=== {{Quote|There is a pouch on your ship that belongs to me.''"
"''So you keep saying. The Jedi stole your stealth suit. I believe she also brought along a walking tank from the Battle of Mizra. I expect it's hiding underneath her bunk.|Narsk Ka'hane and Jarrow Rusher|[[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']]}} [[File:Darthriv.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The outcome of the Battle of Mizra led to the eventual rise in power of Darth Rivan.]] The defeat of the Jedi and Republic forces at the Battle of Mizra was remembered as a game-changing reversal to the sluggish war effort of the Republic. The event caused a particularly notable loss of Republic control over the Outer Rim—one after which it could be argued that centralized Republic [[Politics/Legends|political]] power never returned in that region. After the battle, the Sith also underwent a hardening rise in power, leading to the reign of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]{{DoD|Echoes of the Jedi}} [[Darth Rivan]] on the Expansion Region [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]] and the Dark Lord{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} [[Belia Darzu]] utilizing the half-organic, half-mechanical beings known as [[technobeast]]s during the [[Sictis Wars]] of [[1250 BBY]]–[[1230 BBY]]. In [[1032 BBY/Legends|1032 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']] to [[1032 BBY/Legends|1032 BBY]]. the [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Narsk Ka'hane]] repeatedly asked the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Jarrow Rusher]]—who was knowledgeable about military history—about retrieving his [[Cyricept Personal Concealment System, Mark VI]] [[Stealth suit/Legends|stealth suit]], which had fallen into the possession of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kerra Holt]] and subsequently had ended up aboard Rusher's [[Warship/Legends|warship]] [[Diligence (New Sith Wars)|''Diligence'']]. In response, the disbelieving Rusher quipped that he also thought that Holt had brought on-board and hidden underneath her [[Bunk/Legends|bunk]] a "walking [[Tank/Legends|tank]]" from the Battle of Mizra. ===Written accounts=== {{Quote|The so-called New Sith Wars lasted a thousand years. Many warlords during that time sought to control the destiny of the Sith Empire. They achieved victories from the Battle of Mizra to the Sictus ''[sic]'' Wars, but those were victories without a clear line of succession.|Darth Bane, ''The Rule of Two''|''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''}} [[File:Star_Wars_The_Jedi_Path_Vault.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Battle of Mizra was mentioned in the book ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''.]] The Battle of Mizra was later mentioned in several documents, as well. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Sabla-Mandibu]] noted the reversal of the Jedi fortune in the battle in a chapter of the [[990 BBY]]{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-1.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 1 of 4)}} [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''. At some point between 1000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and [[980 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' and ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' date the events of the latter work to [[980 BBY]]. the Dark Lord of the Sith''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] also namedropped the Battle of Mizra in his work ''[[The Rule of Two]]''. Bane recognized the event as one of the Sith victories of the New Sith Wars but characterized it as a victory "without a clear line of succession," scorning the constant Sith infighting that took place both before and after the battle. Finally, the [[Poet/Legends|poet]] [[Felloux]] immortalized the battle by describing how the drawn and ignited [[Sith lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] of the "scores" of Sith Lords riding speeders during the engagement illuminated the ash-[[Color/Legends|blackened]] sky above the battlefield. In Felloux's characterization, the whining of the speeders drowned out the screams of the dying. Eventually, the Battle of Mizra and its description by Felloux were also noted in a [[36 ABY]] [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|chronicle]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Mizra was first mentioned in the [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} *[[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] *''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2005/10/endnotes-for-star-wars-new-essential.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The New Essential Chronology}} {{NewSithWarsNav}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Mizra |en=Batalla de Mizra |pl=Bitwa o Mizrę }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mizra, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of the New Sith Wars]]
